title: Loops & Control Flow
description: Conditional branching and iteration constructs: if/elif/else, for loops over iterables, while loops, and loop control with break, continue, and else clauses.

sections.title: Conditional Branching (if/elif/else)
sections.description: Evaluate conditions sequentially and execute the first matching block. Python also supports inline ternary conditionals.
sections.codeBlock:
# Standard if-elif-else chain
score = 85
if score >= 90:
    grade = "A"
elif score >= 80:
    grade = "B"
else:
    grade = "C"
print(grade)         # B

# Inline Ternary expression (Ternary Operator)
status = "pass" if score >= 60 else "fail"
print(status)        # pass

sections.title: Sequence Iteration (for, range)
sections.description: The for loop iterates directly over any iterable sequence. The range function generates arithmetic progressions lazily.

sections.title: Advanced Iteration Patterns (enumerate, zip)
sections.description: Use enumerate to loop with an index track, and zip to step through multiple iterables concurrently.
sections.codeBlock:
# enumerate - returns (index, value)
for idx, val in enumerate(["a", "b", "c"]):
    print(idx, val)   # 0 a  1 b  2 c

# zip - iterates in parallel (stops at shortest)
names = ["Alice", "Bob"]
scores = [85, 92]
for n, s in zip(names, scores):
    print(n, s)

sections.title: Indefinite Iteration (while)
sections.description: A while loop continues iterating as long as its condition evaluates to True.
sections.codeBlock:
# Loop with control expression
count = 0
while count < 3:
    print(count)
    count += 1

sections.title: Loop Control (break, continue, else, pass)
sections.description: Alter iteration behaviors using break and continue. The else block executes only if the loop runs to completion without hitting a break.

details:
Python's control flow is distinguished by its use of indentation rather than braces to delimit blocks. The standard is four spaces per level (PEP 8). Mixing tabs and spaces causes a TabError — configure your editor to convert tabs to spaces.

The if/elif/else chain evaluates conditions in order and executes the first true branch. Unlike C-family switch statements, there is no fall-through. The ternary expression x if cond else y provides inline conditional logic; for complex branching, consider match/case (Python 3.10+, structural pattern matching).

The for loop iterates directly over any iterable — lists, strings, dicts, sets, generators, files. It does not use index variables by default; use enumerate() when you need both index and value, and zip() to iterate over multiple sequences in parallel. The range() function generates arithmetic progressions without materializing the full list (lazy evaluation in Python 3).

Loop control: break exits the innermost loop immediately; continue skips the rest of the current iteration. The optional else clause on a loop executes only if the loop completed normally (i.e., no break was hit) — useful for search-and-found patterns.

The while loop repeats as long as its condition is truthy. Be careful with infinite loops — ensure the condition eventually becomes false. The pass statement is a no-op used as a syntactic placeholder where Python requires a block (function body, class body, except clause).

For more detail, see the official control flow tutorial and the compound statements reference.
